creator: grahamperrin@freebsd.org
text:
Created attachment 230523
Screenshot: section 15.5

(In reply to Graham Perrin from comment #4)

With non-collapsing tables of contents at, for example, 
<https://docs.freebsd.org/en/books/porters-handbook/book/> it becomes easier to identify other points where the bug might bite. For example: 

<https://docs.freebsd.org/en/books/porters-handbook/order/#porting-order-broken>

* that is, coincidentally,    /#porting-order-broken 
  as an anchor to where              order is broken 
  within the table of contents :-)
